claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over; Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by gathering some basic information.

Do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 21 now, but I wanted to check if I might still be eligible for Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To help determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to understand his current situation.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He finished his studies a while back.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Let me ask a few more questions to fully assess your situation.

Does Alex live with you, and are you responsible for him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't live with me anymore. He moved out, but I do still help him out financially when I can - you know, give him a bit of money each week to help with his expenses.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing that information. Before I make a final assessment, let me read the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Based on the information you've provided and the official guidance, I now have enough information to make an assessment.

Let me clarify one final point: you mentioned you give Alex money each week - is this amount at least equal to or more than what you would receive in Child Benefit (which is currently around £25 per week for one child)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, I give him about £20 a week to help him out with things he needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. I now have all the details I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry, but based on your circumstances, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why: Child Benefit is available for children under 16, or for young people aged 16-19 (under 20) if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 and is not currently in approved education or training, he falls outside the age criteria for Child Benefit.

While it's commendable that you continue to support Alex financially, the provisions about making contributions when a child lives elsewhere only apply when the basic eligibility criteria (the age and education/training requirements) are still met.

If Alex were to return to approved education or training in the future, you still wouldn't be eligible as he's already over the maximum age limit of 20 for Child Benefit purposes.

Is there anything else I can help clarify about Child Benefit eligibility?

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit is available for children under 16, or for those aged 16-19 if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 and not in approved education or training, he falls outside the age criteria for Child Benefit. Even though you contribute £20 per week towards his expenses, this does not extend eligibility beyond the age limits set by the rules. The provisions about contributions when a child lives elsewhere apply only when the basic eligibility criteria (age and education/training status) are still met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and not in approved education or training. He does not live with the claimant. While the claimant provides financial support of £20 per week, Alex exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility (under 20 and in approved education/training). Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit is available for children under 16, or for those aged 16-19 if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 and not in approved education or training, he falls outside the age criteria for Child Benefit. Even though you contribute £20 per week towards his expenses, this does not extend eligibility beyond the age limits set by the rules. The provisions about contributions when a child lives elsewhere apply only when the basic eligibility criteria (age and education/training status) are still met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and not in approved education or training. He does not live with the claimant. While the claimant provides financial support of £20 per week, Alex exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility (under 20 and in approved education/training). Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
